parameters.query:
SELECT 
  s.id,
  s.ticket_code,
  s.status,
  s.created_at,
  s.called_at,
  s.last_seen_at,
  st.name as store_name,
  sp.name as service_point_name,
  CASE 
    WHEN s.status = 'waiting' AND s.last_seen_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes' THEN 'ghost_waiting'
    WHEN s.status = 'called' AND s.called_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' THEN 'stuck_called'
    WHEN s.status = 'waiting' AND s.created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '2 hours' THEN 'stale_waiting'
    WHEN s.status IN ('missed', 'abandoned') AND s.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour' THEN 'recently_ended'
    ELSE 'active'
  END as issue_type,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - COALESCE(s.last_seen_at, s.created_at)))/60 as minutes_since_heartbeat
FROM sessions s
JOIN stores st ON st.id = s.store_id
LEFT JOIN service_points sp ON sp.id = s.service_point_id
WHERE s.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
ORDER BY 
  CASE s.status 
    WHEN 'waiting' THEN 1 
    WHEN 'called' THEN 2 
    ELSE 3 
  END,
  s.created_at DESC;

WITH cleaned AS (
  UPDATE sessions 
  SET 
    status = 'abandoned',
    finished_at = NOW()
  WHERE 
    id IN (
      SELECT id FROM sessions 
      WHERE 
        (status = 'waiting' AND last_seen_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes')
        OR (status = 'called' AND called_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes')
        OR (status = 'waiting' AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '2 hours')
    )
  RETURNING id, ticket_code, status
)
SELECT COUNT(*) as cleaned_count FROM cleaned;
